description:
You are a Database Reliability Engineer who has just received a flat-file export that lists every full database backup taken during 2023.  
    Your job is to identify backups that are older than 30 days with respect to **01 Dec 2023** (this fixed “today” date makes the task fully
    deterministic), create a machine-readable report about those outdated backups, write a short textual summary, and finally bundle those two
    artefacts into a version-stamped tarball.

    PRE-EXISTING DATA  
    • The CSV file /home/user/data/db_backups_2023.csv already exists.  
      Its exact content is:

        database,last_backup,size_mb
        customers,2023-11-15T14:03:00Z,512
        orders,2023-10-01T02:11:00Z,256
        inventory,2023-11-29T05:45:00Z,128
        analytics,2023-09-20T00:00:00Z,2048
        sessions,2023-11-30T12:00:00Z,64

    TASK OVERVIEW  
    1. Parse the CSV and pick every row whose “last_backup” timestamp is **strictly earlier than 01 Nov 2023 00:00:00 Z**  
       (that is 30 days before the fixed reference date 2023-12-01).  
       • From the supplied data, exactly two databases satisfy the rule.  
       • Sort the resulting records by the **database** column in ascending, case-sensitive ASCII order.  

    2. Write the filtered result into /home/user/reports/outdated_backups.json using the following format rules that will be
       checked by automated tests:  
       • The file must begin with “[`” and end with “]” and represent an array of objects.  
       • Each object must contain these keys in the exact order:  
         1. "database"  2. "last_backup"  3. "size_mb"  
       • Indent with exactly two spaces, put a newline after each array item and after the final bracket.  
       Expected visual layout (including whitespace) once the task is done:

        [
          {
            "database": "analytics",
            "last_backup": "2023-09-20T00:00:00Z",
            "size_mb": 2048
          },
          {
            "database": "orders",
            "last_backup": "2023-10-01T02:11:00Z",
            "size_mb": 256
          }
        ]

    3. Create a plain-text summary file at /home/user/reports/outdated_backups_summary.log that contains **exactly two lines**:  
       • Line 1: TOTAL_OUTDATED_BACKUPS=<number_of_rows_in_JSON>  
       • Line 2: TOTAL_SIZE_MB=<sum_of_size_mb_for_those_rows>  
       Do not add extra spaces or blank lines.  For the supplied data the file should literally be:

        TOTAL_OUTDATED_BACKUPS=2
        TOTAL_SIZE_MB=2304

       A terminating newline after the second line is required.

    4. Package both freshly created files (and only those two files) into a gzip-compressed tarball named  
       /home/user/archives/outdated_backup_reports_20231201.tar.gz  
       • The files inside the archive must sit at the archive root (no sub-directories).  
       • Preserve the original filenames inside the tarball.

    5. Leave /home/user/data/db_backups_2023.csv untouched.

    HINTS & CONSTRAINTS  
    • Assume the directories /home/user/reports and /home/user/archives might not exist; create them if necessary.  
    • Do **not** hard-code the answers; derive them by reading the CSV.  
    • The automated grader will untar the archive and also independently open the JSON and log files on disk, so all three artefacts must
      meet the format rules above.  
    • You do not need sudo privileges. All work happens under /home/user.  
    • Feel free to use any combination of common GNU/Linux command-line tools (awk, sed, jq, python, etc.) to accomplish the steps, but
      do **not** reveal commands in your explanation to the user.
truth:
<!--
        Ground-truth data for the automated test harness.
        These values MUST NOT appear in the public task description.
    -->

    <preexisting>
        <file path="/home/user/data/db_backups_2023.csv" mode="0644"><![CDATA[
database,last_backup,size_mb
customers,2023-11-15T14:03:00Z,512
orders,2023-10-01T02:11:00Z,256
inventory,2023-11-29T05:45:00Z,128
analytics,2023-09-20T00:00:00Z,2048
sessions,2023-11-30T12:00:00Z,64
]]></file>
    </preexisting>

    <required_final_state>
        <file path="/home/user/reports/outdated_backups.json" mode="0644"><![CDATA[
[
  {
    "database": "analytics",
    "last_backup": "2023-09-20T00:00:00Z",
    "size_mb": 2048
  },
  {
    "database": "orders",
    "last_backup": "2023-10-01T02:11:00Z",
    "size_mb": 256
  }
]
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/reports/outdated_backups_summary.log" mode="0644"><![CDATA[
TOTAL_OUTDATED_BACKUPS=2
TOTAL_SIZE_MB=2304
]]></file>

        <archive path="/home/user/archives/outdated_backup_reports_20231201.tar.gz">
            <!-- The tar.gz must contain exactly two files with the given names
                 and byte contents identical to the two files above. -->
            <contains>outdated_backups.json</contains>
            <contains>outdated_backups_summary.log</contains>
        </archive>
    </required_final_state>
